title: Airfoils & Finite Wing Theory
summary: Thin airfoil theory gives the foundation for Cl(α) and the Kutta condition. Prandtl's lifting-line theory shows how finite aspect ratio creates induced drag and reduces effective angle of attack.

keyEquations.label: Thin Airfoil Lift
keyEquations.description: Ideal lift slope 2π per radian for thin airfoils

keyEquations.label: Finite Wing Lift Slope
keyEquations.description: Lifting line theory correction for 3D effects

keyEquations.label: Induced Drag
keyEquations.description: Oswald efficiency e accounts for non-elliptical loading

objectives: Generate NACA 4-digit airfoil shapes and see camber/thickness effects
Apply thin airfoil theory to predict Cl vs α
Use lifting-line theory to compute finite-wing Cl, Cd_i for varying AR and taper
Understand why high AR wings have lower induced drag
